page: Amazon Web Services m8i.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 16 vCPUs (8 physical cores and 2 threads per core) operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supported by a 480 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8i.4xlarge demonstrates solid performance across multiple domains. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2118.0, which is categorized as strong, and a multi-core score of 13263.0. Single-threaded operations perform well, with gzip compression reaching 34.08 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.43 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 25%. Text processing is a standout capability, scoring 2932.0 in Geekbench, placing it in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth is measured at 115.07 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 67.48 GB/sec for large-block write operations.

The server's hardware profile offers balanced resource density, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads requiring a steady mix of compute and memory without the overhead of specialized accelerators. It is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 28.77 GB/sec, and database operations, scoring 8702.86 in Passmark database tests. Additionally, the CPU's advanced vector and matrix extensions support light machine learning tasks, as demonstrated by an LLM text generation speed of 328.57 tokens/sec for a small 135M model.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server powered by a dedicated 16-vCPU Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory, a 480 MB L3 cache, and a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong single-threaded performance, with gzip compression at 34.08 GB/sec and top-tier text processing capabilities. The server delivers balanced resource density, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for general-purpose applications. It is highly suitable for workloads such as static web serving, database operations, and light machine learning inference utilizing its built-in Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.4xlarge is a general-purpose server with 16 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU. It delivers strong single-threaded performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.4xlarge is a general-purpose Nitro instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for diverse enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated 16-vCPU Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz
64.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Strong single-threaded performance and top-tier text processing capabilities
Built-in Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for light machine learning inference
Optimized for static web serving, database operations, and general-purpose workloads
